stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Wharton Esherick (1887–1970) was an American sculptor and furniture maker.
  • He trained as a painter before turning to woodworking in the 1920s.
  • He received the Gold Medal from the American Institute of Architects in 1958.
  • The Philadelphia Museum of Art owns a music stand that Esherick built from cherry wood.
  • The stand has three curving legs that taper to delicate points and a slender, asymmetrical top.
stem: The student wants to describe the music stand to an audience unfamiliar with Wharton Esherick. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Wharton Esherick lived from 1887 to 1970, and a music stand he made can be seen at the Philadelphia Museum of Art.

With its three curving legs and slender, asymmetrical top, the cherry-wood music stand in the Philadelphia Museum of Art is one of many works created by American sculptor and furniture maker Wharton Esherick.
Sculptor and furniture maker Wharton Esherick received the Gold Medal from the American Institute of Architects in 1958.
The music stand is made from cherry wood and has three curving legs that taper to points and a slender, asymmetrical top.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence describes the music stand for an audience unfamiliar with Esherick, mentioning its curving legs and asymmetrical top while identifying Esherick (the stand’s maker) as an American sculptor and furniture maker.

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence identifies Esherick and mentions the music stand, it doesn’t describe the stand. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence identifies Esherick but doesn’t describe the music stand. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence describes the music stand but doesn’t identify Esherick for the unfamiliar audience.
